sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The animation frames all show the same colors - the animation didn't actually change the colors, it just highlighted/flashed the 4 corner cells. The 4 corners of the bottom-right grid are:
- (36,36): R
- (36,52): b
- (52,36): b
- (52,52): R
So clicking on the top-left grid highlights the 4 corners of the bottom-right grid. This might be showing which cells need attention or it's some kind of selection mechanism.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the top grids are "templates" and clicking on them copies their pattern to the bottom-right grid. Or maybe clicking on different cells of the top grids highlights different parts of the bottom-right grid.
Let me try clicking on different cells of the top-left grid to see what gets highlighted in the bottom-right grid. For example, clicking on the top-middle cell of top-left might highlight the top-middle cell of bottom-right.
Actually, let me try a different approach. Let me click on the center cell of the top-left grid and see what happens.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model: Clicking top-left grid highlights 4 corner cells of bottom-right grid (transient animation). This suggests clicking on source grids selects/highlights corresponding cells in the target grid. Need to test more click positions to understand the mapping.
